title: Multi-MB pages from base64-inlined hero images crush mobile/limited-data students
evidence: First-hand HTML analysis of all 154 pages >500KB in /crawl found ~9 pages shipping >100KB of base64-encoded images embedded directly in the HTML document (not as cacheable image files): finearts-music.unimelb.edu.au/about-us/news/meet-nicole-goode,-honours-visual-art-graduate = 2,292KB total, 2,071KB is a SINGLE inlined photo; mdhs.unimelb.edu.au/diversity-and-inclusion/initiatives/dei-conference = 2,704KB (2,501KB base64); msd.unimelb.edu.au/thus/news-events/urban-density-and-access-to-social-services = 1,779KB (1,617KB); mdhs .../mdhs-diversity-and-inclusion-grants = 1,220KB (1,054KB); law.unimelb.edu.au/news/MLS/from-education-to-precision-use = 1,145KB (976KB); biomedicalsciences .../cmr/about-us/contact-us = 734KB (570KB). page-weight.csv confirms these as the per-domain max outliers (finearts max 2,292KB, medicine 2,516KB, msd 1,779KB). Base64 inlining defeats browser caching, lazy-loading, responsive srcset, and CDN image optimization, and inflates the HTML ~33% (encoding overhead) — every byte is re-downloaded on every visit. On a 3G/limited-data plan a 2MB news article is a ~10–20s wait and a real data cost.
recommendation: Add a CMS publish-time guard (and a crawl-based linter over freshness-signals tooling) that blocks/flags any inline data:image payload over ~10KB; convert offending images to served files with width-appropriate srcset, loading="lazy", and modern formats (WebP/AVIF). Run a one-off sweep to de-inline the ~9 known >100KB offenders. Set a hard page-weight budget (e.g. 500KB HTML) enforced in CI so editors cannot paste large base64 blobs from Word/design tools again.

title: Cookiebot external-link-arrow icon duplicated ~1,925× per page inflates staff pages to 5MB
evidence: law.unimelb.edu.au staff-listing pages (e.g. /mls-staff-listing-records/.../luis-eslava/luis-eslava and 3 sibling profiles) are 5,007–5,056KB each — but contain almost NO real content. Analysis shows 1,926 identical tags, all carrying the SAME ~400-byte base64 PNG (class CybotExternalLinkArrow, the Cookiebot 'opens in new window' arrow), i.e. the same string repeated 1,926 times instead of referenced once. mdhs.unimelb.edu.au/research/research-training/supervisors/admissionsscholarships/times-higher-education-world-university-rankings = 4,730KB with 1,924 copies. These are the absolute max outliers in page-weight.csv (law max 5,056KB, mdhs max 4,730KB). gzip masks but does not fix it (the law page still parses 5MB of DOM = 1,938 nodes the phone must build). Root cause is the Cookiebot consent script re-decorating every external link per render combined with these pages embedding an external page's link soup.
recommendation: Quick config fix: configure Cookiebot to reference the external-link-arrow as a single shared sprite/CSS background (or disable its auto-icon and use a CSS ::after), eliminating ~770KB+ of duplicated base64 per affected page instantly. Audit the law 'mls-staff-listing-records' template — it appears to embed full external-site markup; strip to the actual profile content. This is the cheapest high-impact win in the estate: a settings change drops 4 law pages from ~5MB to well under 200KB.

title: Student course template ships ~327KB of document weight before a single external file loads
evidence: The study.unimelb /find/courses/ template is the highest-traffic student-facing page type (course structure/entry/how-to-apply). Sampling 25 such pages: median HTML 327KB, of which ~109KB inline SVG, ~79KB inline JS, ~43KB inline CSS — i.e. ~70%+ of the document is inlined assets, not content. The single biggest inline SVG is a 106KB icon sprite sheet (187 <path> icons) embedded in EVERY course page though most icons are never shown. study.unimelb median across all pages is 304KB (page-weight.csv), the heaviest domain by median in the estate; max 1,132KB (master-of-environment/structure). Because it is inlined per-page it cannot be cached across the multi-page course journey (structure → entry-requirements → fees → how-to-apply), so a prospective/continuing student re-downloads the same 106KB sprite + JS + CSS on every step.
recommendation: Externalize the shared icon sprite, base CSS, and base JS into cacheable files referenced (not inlined) so they download once for the whole course journey; keep only above-the-fold critical CSS inline. Tree-shake the icon sprite to icons actually used on course pages. Target: cut the per-page course document from ~327KB to <120KB, with the heavy shared assets cached after the first page. This directly speeds the most common student task flow on mobile.

title: ~18–21 render-blocking external scripts (mostly third-party tags) on key student pages
evidence: Sampling 20 pages/domain for external resource counts: study.unimelb course pages load a median of 10 external stylesheets + 21 external <script src> with only ~13% carrying defer/async (so ~18 scripts block parsing); students.unimelb, medicine, msd, eng all run 15–22 external scripts at 0% defer/async. The study course page's external scripts are dominated by third-party tag/analytics: 7× googletagmanager.com, google-analytics.com, tealiumiq.com + tiqcdn.com (Tealium), cdn.optimizely.com, cdn1.adoberesources.net — render-time tag managers that each pull further scripts. 3 separate font hosts (fonts.googleapis/gstatic + typekit) are referenced on every domain, adding DNS+TLS+FOIT cost. Only ~2 preconnect/preload hints present on the course page despite ~10 distinct origins.
recommendation: Consolidate to a single tag manager (currently both GTM and Tealium load), defer/async all non-critical scripts, and self-host or preconnect the 3 font origins (collapse to one). Add resource hints (preconnect) for the handful of real origins. Move analytics/personalization (Optimizely, Adobe) behind requestIdleCallback so they do not block first paint. This shrinks the mobile request waterfall and Time-to-Interactive without changing any content.

title: No estate-wide performance/page-weight budget or monitoring — outliers go unnoticed
evidence: The 20-domain page-weight.csv shows median HTML ranging 143–304KB but max values exploding to 1.1–5.0MB (law 5,056KB, mdhs 4,730KB, medicine 2,516KB, finearts 2,292KB, msd 1,779KB, arts 1,677KB, fbe 1,415KB) — a 4×–30× gap between median and max within single domains, proof that nothing flags a page when it balloons. The two worst anti-patterns (base64 hero images; 1,925× duplicated Cookiebot icon) are silent CMS/plugin side-effects no editor intended. There is no Core Web Vitals / Lighthouse signal in the corpus and no weight ceiling; the same templates are reused across thousands of pages (template-clones.csv) so a heavy template multiplies estate-wide.
recommendation: Stand up automated performance governance: a scheduled Lighthouse/WebPageTest run on representative templates per domain (course page, news article, staff profile, faculty hub) tracking LCP/INP/CLS + total bytes, plus a CI/publish gate enforcing an HTML weight budget (e.g. warn >500KB, block >1MB). Surface a per-domain weight dashboard so faculty web owners see their max-page outliers. This is the structural fix that prevents the specific 5MB/2MB pages found here from recurring.

title: Deep nav re-ships inline on every page, multiplying mobile cost; one page disables pinch-zoom
evidence: Round1 1.5 established study 58% / medicine 59% / msd 65% of pages sit ≥5 levels deep (nav-depth.csv, max depth 9–11). Because the full multi-level nav tree plus the 106KB icon sprite is rendered INLINE in each document (not lazy/cached), the deep IA directly inflates every page's first-load weight on mobile — the deeper the tree, the more nav markup ships per page, paid on a phone connection on every navigation. Separately, mobile-readiness sampling (40 pages × 15 domains = 600 pages) found viewport meta present on 100% (good baseline) BUT at least one medicine.unimelb page sets user-scalable=no + maximum-scale=1, blocking pinch-zoom — a WCAG 1.4.4/1.4.10 mobile-accessibility failure that prevents low-vision students enlarging text on a phone.
recommendation: (a) Render the global/section nav as a collapsed mobile drawer fed by a cached, externalized component rather than a full inline tree per page, so deep IA does not tax every mobile page-load; pair with breadcrumb truncation for ≥5-level pages. (b) Grep the estate for user-scalable=no / maximum-scale=1 and remove it everywhere so pinch-zoom is never disabled. (c) Because nav weight scales with depth, flattening the worst study/medicine/msd branches (round1 IA work) yields a measurable mobile-performance dividend, not just findability.

title: Collapse the triple-tree URL duplication on core enrolled-student fee pages
evidence: Every core fee page on students.unimelb resolves under 2-3 live URL paths that all canonical to /course-admin/. Verified by reading raw page.html canonical tags: 'paying your fees' is served at /course-admin/paying-your-fees (canonical), /your-course/manage-your-course/fees-and-payments/paying-your-fees, AND /admin/fees/pay-my-fees; census-dates at /course-admin/census-dates + /your-course/manage-your-course/subject-enrolment/census-dates; fee-remission-in-special-circumstances at /course-admin/... + /your-course/manage-your-course/fees-and-payments/...; understanding-your-fees crawled under 2 hashes (a03988dbd10392be, ae3aeaea62ab6103), both 264491 bytes. Internal UoM links disagree on which path to use: study.unimelb fee hubs link to the /course-admin/ canonical, but students.unimelb pages link each other via /your-course/... and /admin/.... Files: crawl/students-full/pages/{f00f48ccfe17893d,13e8a47c25f18c53,843354a8b0ab74d0,a6d72d7b29fd9eed}/page.html.
recommendation: Pick /course-admin/ as the single canonical fee tree and 301-redirect the /your-course/manage-your-course/fees-and-payments/ and /admin/fees/ duplicates to it (don't just rely on rel=canonical — the duplicates are still independently linked and indexable). Then sweep internal links so every UoM page (faculty + study.unimelb + students.unimelb) points at the canonical path. This removes the version-skew risk where two URLs for 'paying your fees' can drift out of sync, and consolidates SEO authority onto one URL per fee topic.

title: Fix broken/mismatched metadata on the highest-stakes fee and census pages
evidence:
Read directly from raw HTML: /admin/census-dates (crawl/students-full/pages/721780140fbbfba1/page.html) declares rel=canonical href=https://students.unimelb.edu.au/page-not-found — a census page (the legally binding fee/withdrawal deadline) canonicalised to a 404. The /student-support/scholarships-and-financial-support/financial-aid page (cd1388552eb23434/page.html) renders Support and wellbeing | Current students and

Support and wellbeing

— neither the title nor H1 mentions fees, financial aid, or its own slug, so it is unfindable by search and ambiguous in results. These are the exact pages a fee-stressed student searches for.
recommendation: Correct the /admin/census-dates canonical to the real /course-admin/census-dates target, and give the financial-aid page a descriptive, slug-matching <title>/<h1> ('Financial aid and emergency support' or similar). Audit all 17 students.unimelb fee/finance pages for canonical-to-404 and generic-title defects as a quick batch fix; these are high-traffic, high-consequence pages where a wrong census date or an unfindable hardship page has real student impact.

title: Migrate core fee answers off the legacy ask.unimelb FAQ system into the maintained IA
evidence: The flagship enrolled fee explainer /course-admin/understanding-your-fees (crawl/students-full/pages/a03988dbd10392be/links.json, 61 links) outsources 16+ substantive fee answers to the legacy ask.unimelb.edu.au knowledge base: Fees Due Dates (a_id/925), Overdue Fees Penalty (a_id/934), What is FEE-HELP (a_id/914), Applying for HECS-HELP (a_id/5179), Fee Date Extensions (a_id/931), Fee Paying Instalments (a_id/926), Fee Account Statement (a_id/6007), refunds (a_id/946), CSP Payment Options (a_id/923), SSAF (a_id/5570), SA-HELP (a_id/5566). Across students.unimelb, 96 pages link into ask.unimelb /app/answers. The same FAQ is linked with inconsistent formats and protocols on one page: http://ask.unimelb.edu.au/app/answers/detail/a_id/5570/ AND https://ask.unimelb.edu.au/faq/5570/. This makes the deadline/penalty/refund rules — the answers students most need — live in a separate, differently-templated, partly-http system outside the governed students.unimelb IA.
recommendation: Bring the high-traffic fee answers (due dates, penalties, FEE-HELP/HECS, instalments, refunds, fee statements, SSAF) inline into students.unimelb fee pages, or at minimum normalise every ask.unimelb link to a single https /faq/<id>/ format and put the legacy KB behind a documented deprecation/redirect plan. Owning the deadline and penalty content in the maintained IA reduces the chance of stale fee rules and gives one authoritative answer per question.

title: Build a single enrolled-student 'Fees and finance' hub and stop sending enrolled students to study.unimelb
evidence: Reverse-funnel split confirmed from fees-finance.csv after filtering to 390 genuine fee/finance pages: study.unimelb 355 (almost all per-course /find/courses/graduate/<course>/fees/ applicant templates — 338 of 355) vs students.unimelb only 17. There is no clean single fee landing for enrolled students — fee content is scattered across /course-admin/, /your-course/manage-your-course/fees-and-payments/, /student-support/scholarships-and-financial-support/, and /admin/. study.unimelb applicant fee hubs (/how-to-apply/.../fees-and-payments) carry only 2-5 forward links to enrolled students.unimelb content (verified in study-unimelb-edu-au links.json), so an enrolled student who lands on the marketing fee page has a weak path back. Nav-depth.csv already shows study.unimelb buries 58% of pages >=5 levels deep.
recommendation: Create one canonical enrolled 'Fees and finance' hub on students.unimelb (under /course-admin/) that links out to: pay my fees (my.unimelb), understanding your fees, census dates, FEE-HELP/HECS eligibility, fee remission/refunds, financial aid/hardship, and scholarship payments — so enrolled students never need to traverse the applicant-facing study.unimelb fee templates. Keep study.unimelb fee pages scoped to applicants/cost-to-study, and add a clear 'already enrolled? go here' bridge from each applicant fee page to the enrolled hub.

title: Consolidate the cross-domain census-date duplication into one authoritative source
evidence: arts.unimelb.edu.au/students/graduate-coursework/census-dates (crawl/arts-unimelb-edu-au) hosts a census-dates page whose rel=canonical points to the central students.unimelb.edu.au/course-admin/census-dates — i.e. a faculty domain is maintaining its own copy of the single most legally significant date in the student lifecycle (census = last day to withdraw without fee/academic penalty). Any drift between the faculty copy and the central page produces a wrong-deadline risk. Within that arts page the internal links also mix /course-admin/census-dates and /your-course/manage-your-course/subject-enrolment/census-dates, compounding the canonical confusion documented in finding 1.
recommendation: Establish students.unimelb /course-admin/census-dates as the sole authoritative census source and convert the arts faculty census page (and any other faculty fee/census stubs surfaced by this pattern) to a redirect or a thin pointer, never a content copy. Add a governance rule that census dates, fee deadlines, and FEE-HELP rules are central-only and may not be duplicated onto faculty domains.

title: Surface and strengthen the financial-hardship / emergency-support and fee-remission pathway
evidence: Full-text scan of all 833 crawled students.unimelb pages: 'financial hardship' appears on only 5 pages and 'bursary' on just 2 — very thin coverage of the pathway a fee-stressed student actively searches for. The fee-remission/refund route funnels into a single external form forms.your.unimelb.edu.au/4747166 reached via three opaque SID query params on the fee-remission page (SID=...fbZ waiver, ...fdB review, ...fZx enquiry); 143 students.unimelb pages reference that form id. The financial-aid page itself is mis-titled 'Support and wellbeing' (see finding 2), so the hardship content that does exist is hard to find. _nocache? URL variants of fee pages also leak into the link graph (15 pages link to a _nocache version, e.g. the CSP/HELP-loans eligibility page).
recommendation: Create a clearly-titled, findable 'Financial difficulty / emergency support' page that consolidates fee remission, fee-payment extensions, hardship grants/bursaries, emergency loans, and Stop 1 financial advice into one entry point, and link it prominently from the fees hub, the census page, and paying-your-fees. Label the three fee-remission form actions in plain language rather than relying on indistinguishable SID links, and strip _nocache query-string variants from internal links so the hardship/eligibility pages present a single clean URL.

title: International-compliance content is forked across three live trees; consolidate to one canonical CoE/visa/OSHC/study-load journey
evidence: Estate-wide inbound index (scratchpad/inbound_index.json, built from 27,165 links.json across 19 domains) shows the international-compliance subtree exists three times: Tree A /support-and-wellbeing/international-student-support (465 inbound, 27 pages), Tree B /student-support/international-student-support + /support-for-international-students (772 inbound, 22 pages), Tree C /sandbox/2026-uplifts/student-visas (90 inbound, 12 pages). 862 inbound links point at the deprecated B + draft C trees instead of A. Same task duplicated under different URLs/labels e.g. 'keep visa details current' lives at A:.../meet-your-student-visa-conditions/keep-your-details-up-to-date (32 inbound), B:.../visas/updating-your-visa-status (the round-1 2.9 'orphan', 1 inbound) and C:.../keep-your-personal-and-visa-details-up-to-date (8 inbound). OSHC, CoE-request and enrolment-change pages all triplicate similarly. This is the single highest-consequence current-student journey (immigration risk) and round 1 (2.9) flagged only ONE orphan within it, never the 3-tree fork.
recommendation: Run a canonical decision specifically for the international-compliance subtree (do NOT inherit round-1 1.1's blanket '/support-and-wellbeing wins' rule — see the canonicalization-conflict finding). Pick ONE canonical URL per task (CoE request, apply/renew/extend visa, OSHC, meet visa conditions, manage enrolment changes, reduced study load, ESOS, transfer), 301 the other two trees' equivalents onto it, add rel=canonical, and publish a redirect-coverage check so no compliance task has live inbound on >1 tree. Acceptance test: re-run the inbound index; target 0 compliance pages reachable on 2+ trees (currently ~20).

title: A draft /sandbox/2026-uplifts/student-visas tree is live, indexed, and accumulating production links on immigration-critical content
evidence: freshness-signals.csv flags 13 students.unimelb /sandbox URLs; 12 are under /sandbox/2026-uplifts/student-visas/. These are NOT empty stubs — page.html for /sandbox/2026-uplifts/student-visas/support-with-student-visas is 812,250 bytes with 19,493 chars of real visible text covering OSHC, CoE, study load and 'Department of Home Affairs'. The draft tree has pulled 90 real inbound links (e.g. .../arrange-your-overseas-student-health-cover-oshc 8 inbound with correct anchor text from students.unimelb itself), so editors are already linking to staging. One URL (.../renew-your-student-visa/_nocache) resolves to a 'University of Melbourne - Sign In' wall, confirming it is a live staging surface, not a content model. A parallel /sandbox/content-models/student-equity-and-disability-services tree also leaks (its SEDS-privacy page has 10 inbound). Round 1 noted sandbox/draft pages generically (freshness) but never that the DRAFT visa tree is link-reachable for the highest-risk topic.
recommendation: Immediately noindex + robots-block /sandbox/* and return 404/redirect for the 13 students.unimelb sandbox URLs, or move the 2026-uplift visa rewrite into the canonical tree and retire the sandbox path. Add a publish-gate/hook so /sandbox and /staging paths cannot acquire inbound links from production pages. Audit the 90 inbound links and repoint them to the canonical compliance pages.

title: Disability/equity registration wizard exists on only one of three equity hubs; faculties route students to the two without it
evidence: The disability/equity service hub is forked across three names: /support-and-wellbeing/student-equity-and-disability-services (137 inbound), /student-support/student-equity-and-disability-services (63), /student-support/student-equity-and-disability-support (31). The 4-step register-for-support wizard (prepare-to-register → registration-for-ongoing-support → create-your-plan → action-your-plan, each ~10-16 inbound with 'Step N' anchors) exists ONLY under /support-and-wellbeing/...services. Yet 12 external domains (arts, biomedicalsciences, education, eng, fbe, handbook, law, mdhs, medicine, msd, services, study) link disabled students to the two /student-support hubs that do NOT carry the wizard steps. A SEDS privacy page is additionally served from /sandbox/content-models. Round 1 mentioned an 'equity registration twin' only in passing under 1.1's generic 120-leaf parallel-tree count; it never identified that the actionable registration path is missing from the hubs faculties actually link to. Disability cohorts least absorb this fragmentation.
recommendation: Canonicalize to one equity/disability hub that carries the full register wizard; 301 the other two hub names onto it; add rel=canonical. Fix the 12 external faculty/service links to point at the wizard hub (or its step 1). Ensure every equity hub variant either redirects or surfaces the prepare→register→create→action steps above the fold. Acceptance: 0 inbound from faculties landing on an equity hub without a visible 'register for ongoing support' path.

title: Indigenous students have no presence on the current-students hub; support is offloaded to a separate, fragmented, www-split set of domains
evidence: A scan of all 833 students.unimelb pages returns ZERO Indigenous-specific pages — Murrup Barak (Melbourne's Indigenous student support unit) is not in the current-students IA at all. It lives on a separate, uncrawled domain murrupbarak.unimelb.edu.au, reached only as an outbound destination, and the volume is lopsided: msd 614, study 79, mdhs 49, but students.unimelb only 19-35 (cross-site-flow.csv / domain-edges.csv). Indigenous support is itself scattered across 4+ destinations — murrupbarak (student support), indigenousknowledge.unimelb (research institute, 90 from research), Wilin Centre for Indigenous Arts (vca/finearts, 29-10), and indigenousscholarships.com.au — with no single canonical Indigenous-student path. Anchors drift ('Indigenous Students' 611x vs 'Murrup Barak' 47x vs 'Visit Murrup Barak website' 5x) and the destination is www-split (murrupbarak vs www.murrupbarak, per register 5.7's 28 www-twins). Round 1 / D6 named Indigenous support as an uncovered journey but produced no finding.
recommendation: Create a canonical Indigenous-students landing page within students.unimelb (mirroring the international-student-support hub pattern) that introduces Murrup Barak, scholarships, and cultural-leave, then deep-links out — so Indigenous students enter through the same front door as every other cohort instead of being bounced to an external microsite. Standardise the outbound anchor to one label, resolve the murrupbarak/www.murrupbarak twin to one host, and have faculties link to the new students.unimelb hub page rather than 5 different Indigenous destinations.

title: Find→act break: the two highest-immigration-risk compliance pages link to zero transactional forms
evidence: Parsing links.json for the canonical compliance pages: /support-and-wellbeing/international-student-support/visas/meet-your-student-visa-conditions/manage-enrolment-changes-that-affect-your-visa and /support-and-wellbeing/international-student-support/reduced-study-load each contain NO links to any *.app.unimelb, forms.your.unimelb, *form.unimelb or my.unimelb transactional host — despite their bodies (17,599 and 11,048 visible chars) explicitly warning about CoE cancellation, full-time-load breach and census dates. By contrast the CoE-request page does link forms.your x1 and the equity registration page links specialconsideration.app x2, proving the in-page CTA pattern exists and is simply absent on the two pages where a wrong move has immigration consequences. This is distinct from round-1 6.7/3.2 (generic catalogue→my.unimelb wiring) and is the acute end of the find→act gap for the riskiest decisions.
recommendation: Add an explicit, prominent in-page action on both pages: 'Apply to reduce your study load / vary enrolment →' deep-linking to enrolmentvariations.app (or the special-consideration/compassionate-circumstances form) and a 'Talk to the Student Visa Team →' Stop 1 contact. Establish a rule that every compliance page describing a visa-affecting action must carry the matching transactional link. Cheap, high-impact: ~2 page edits closing the costliest seam in the estate.

title: Canonicalization direction for compliance/equity subtrees conflicts with round-1's blanket rule; needs an evidence-led, documented canonical map
evidence: Round-1 finding 1.1 prescribes canonicalizing the 120 parallel leaves TO /support-and-wellbeing and /your-course as 'the newer targets'. But for the two highest-risk subtrees the directional pull from EXTERNAL domains is the opposite: the deprecated /student-support international tree carries 772 inbound vs 465 for /support-and-wellbeing, and ALL 11 external faculty/service domains link to /student-support; for OSHC specifically, services(5), study(4), mdhs(4), fbe(1), eng(1), education(1) all point at the deprecated /student-support/...oshc page. For equity, 12 external domains land on the /student-support hubs. So a naive 'redirect everything to /support-and-wellbeing' would 301 the exact URLs 11-12 faculties rely on, and could orphan the live equity wizard if applied without per-subtree analysis. Round 1 did not surface this conflict because it measured leaf overlap on students.unimelb only, not external-domain link direction.
recommendation: Before executing the 1.1 redirect program, produce a per-subtree canonical map for the compliance and equity journeys using external inbound direction + content completeness (which tree has the full wizard/CoE chain) as tie-breakers, not just 'newest path'. Where the canonical target differs from the faculty-preferred deprecated URL, batch-update the faculty links in the same release as the 301s so no compliance/equity link 301-chains or dead-ends. Record the decision in the governance registry (register 5.x) so the two highest-risk journeys have a named owner and a documented canonical URL.

title: t
evidence: e
recommendation: r

title: Redirect the 18 byte-identical HDR candidature/thesis pages (education+mdhs) to gradresearch.unimelb FIRST; reconcile the drifted remainder separately
evidence: Reproducible via analysis/full-scrape/near_dup.py (k=8 shingle-Jaccard) -> near-duplicates.csv, cluster 4.1_HDR_education_vs_mdhs: of 33 shared-tail cross-domain pairs, EXACTLY 18 score Jaccard=1.000 (byte-identical). Verified genuine substantive content on BOTH domains, not shells: education.unimelb/study/graduate-research/current-students/reviewscandidature-variations/confirmation (3,061 words, 182,471 bytes) is identical to mdhs.unimelb/research/research-training/current-students/.../confirmation (3,061 words, 182,465 bytes); same for thesisexamination/thesis-with-publication (4,202w both), reviewscandidature-variations/unsatisfactory-progress (3,154w both), thesisexamination/writing-my-thesis (2,867w), my-thesis-in-the-library (3,573w), plus 13 more (full list in near-duplicates.csv). Canonical owner already exists (education->gradresearch 1,972 + mdhs->gradresearch 1,882 contextual links per cross-site-flow.csv).
recommendation: Phase the de-dup by the now-measured score, don't treat all 33 as one block. (1) Immediately 301-redirect (or replace with a thin faculty stub) the 18 Jaccard=1.000 pages on BOTH education and mdhs to the single gradresearch.unimelb canonical, since they are provably maintained-in-triplicate identical policy. Worklist = the 18 rows with jaccard=1.0 in near-duplicates.csv cluster 4.1. (2) The ~7 pairs that have DRIFTED (financial-aid 0.143, academic-skills 0.164, overseas-student-health-cover 0.116, renewing-your-student-visa 0.200) are the same nominal page already telling two faculties' HDR candidates different things — reconcile content to one gradresearch version before redirecting, and treat the drift as the concrete cost case for stakeholders. (3) Ignore the 8 dual-shell /policy/* pairs (6-word JS stubs, Jaccard 0.0) — they are not real content. Re-run near_dup.py after migration as the acceptance test (target: 0 pairs at Jaccard>=0.85 in cluster 4.1).

title: Decommission MBS's duplicate BCom careers tree: 301 the 46 confirmed-duplicate /career/ tails (incl. all 35 cross-branded 'fbe' URLs) to fbe.unimelb
evidence: near_dup.py cluster 4.2_BCom_careers_FBE_vs_MBS: FBE exposes 73 tails under /bcom/career/, MBS 71 under /career/, with 59 SHARED tails (reconciles round-1's '59' exactly). Of 58 scored pairs, 46 score Jaccard>=0.85 (median 0.92) — substantially stronger duplication than round-1's '8 of 10 sampled >=0.6'. Highest: mentoring/career-mentoring-program 0.987, expertise/career-discovery 0.987, expertise/digital-industry-projects 0.982, your-community/clubs-and-societies 0.979, fbe-student-calendar 0.963. Cross-branding is literal: 35 MBS /career/ URLs contain 'fbe' (e.g. mbs.unimelb.edu.au/career/fbe-student-calendar and its entire jun/july/aug/sep subtree, /career/your-community/fbe-chats-snacks) — MBS is serving FBE-branded student-calendar and BCom content. Full pair list in near-duplicates.csv.
recommendation: Designate fbe.unimelb.edu.au/bcom/career as the single owner of BCom careers content and 301-redirect the 46 MBS /career/ tails that score >=0.85 to their FBE equivalents (worklist = the 46 jaccard>=0.85 rows in near-duplicates.csv cluster 4.2). Do the 35 cross-branded 'fbe' URLs in the same pass — an MBS host serving 'fbe-student-calendar' is an unambiguous copy-paste-build artifact, not intentional MBS content. For the ~12 tails scoring 0.30-0.85, eyeball whether MBS has localised them (MBS-specific vs BCom-specific) before redirecting; keep only genuinely MBS-program-specific pages. Acceptance test: re-run near_dup.py; cluster 4.2 should drop to only intentionally-distinct pages.

title: Replace the 9 reworded 'Employability' scaffold pages with ONE shared central employability component + thin faculty stubs
evidence: near_dup.py cluster 4.3_Employability_in_X found 9 members (corrects round-1's '7'): the 7 faculty 'Employability in X' pages (arts 1,015w, biomedicalsciences/'Employability in MDHS' 986w, education 1,089w, eng 1,242w, finearts-music 1,011w, msd/'...Architecture Building & Planning' 1,299w, science 1,234w) PLUS 2 business-school 'Employability' pages (fbe 623w, mbs 593w). All 36 pairwise Jaccard scores fall in 0.09-0.52 (max arts<->biomedicalsciences 0.52, biomed<->education 0.50, fbe<->mbs 0.50) — NONE reach 0.85, confirming round-1's read that these are a shared scaffold reworded per discipline, not body-identical. Reproducible in near-duplicates.csv.
recommendation: Because the similarity is structural (0.28-0.52) not literal, do NOT redirect — instead extract the common scaffold (what employability means at Melbourne, links to careers service + mentoring + internship subjects) into ONE central component owned by students.unimelb/central Careers, injected identically into all 9 pages, leaving a thin per-faculty block for discipline-specific internship subjects and contacts. This removes the 9x drift surface that the spread of scores (0.09 to 0.52) already shows is underway. Fold the FBE/MBS 'Employability' pages into this AND the 4.2 BCom consolidation so business-school employability has a single home.

title: Add a standing near-duplicate CI check (near_dup.py) to the web governance toolchain so cross-domain content forks are detected, not discovered
evidence: Before this task, the repo had NO saved near-duplicate detector (critic verify_next #2: 'grep matched only the literal word inside a markdown doc; fingerprint-based dup found exactly 0') — the headline duplication findings rode on an unsaved ad-hoc Jaccard. The estate has at least 3 confirmed cross-domain duplicate clusters (18 identical HDR pages, 46 FBE/MBS career pages, 9 employability scaffolds) totalling ~73 forked pages across 4 faculty CMSes, and the drift visible in cluster 4.1 (financial-aid 0.143, OSHC 0.116) proves copies silently diverge once created. analysis/full-scrape/near_dup.py now reproduces all of this in one command with no network dependency.
recommendation: Promote near_dup.py from a one-off into a scheduled governance check: extend its cluster builders to scan all faculty domains for shared URL path-tails (not just the 3 known clusters), run it nightly/weekly against the crawl, and alert when any cross-domain pair exceeds a drift band (e.g. flag NEW pairs >=0.85 as 'should be one canonical' and EXISTING known-duplicate pairs that have fallen below 0.95 as 'copies have drifted, reconcile'). This operationalises round-1 finding 5.1's subdomain-registry intent at the content layer — it catches the copy-paste site builds (like MBS serving fbe-student-calendar) at publish time instead of two years later in an audit.

title: Correct round-1 finding 4.1 wording before it reaches the deck: '~33 pages at Jaccard~1.0' -> '33 mirror pairs, 18 byte-identical, remainder drifting'
evidence: Round-1 register section 4.1 states 'every sampled pair scored Jaccard=1.000 with matching word counts' and the Top-12 table says '33 path-tails identical (Jaccard=1.000)'. The reproducible run shows this is true for 18 of the 33 shared-tail pairs but NOT the full 33: 8 pairs are dual shells (0.0) and ~7 have genuinely diverged (0.05-0.22). The 'every sampled pair = 1.000' phrasing came from a small sample that happened to hit the identical subset. Evidence: near-duplicates.csv cluster 4.1 (18 rows at exactly 1.0) + the script's console summary (pairs_scored:33, pairs_ge_threshold:18).
recommendation: Edit the round-1 register's 4.1 and Top-12 row to the defensible, reproducible claim: '33 mirror page-pairs across education+mdhs; 18 are byte-identical (Jaccard=1.000) and should be redirected to gradresearch; the remaining 15 split into 8 empty shells and ~7 that have already drifted between faculties — the drift being the maintenance-cost evidence.' Cite analysis/full-scrape/near_dup.py + near-duplicates.csv as the reproducible source so a reviewer can re-run it. This closes the critic's specific reproducibility objection and makes the de-dup ask more credible, not less (drift is a stronger argument than 'they are all identical').
